data.title: Bound SeenSessions sync.Map growth (LRU or TTL)

data.body:
Severity: low (both axes). Source: TYB-12-followup audits.

Where: server/internal/httpserver/checkout_handlers.go:37 (StripeSetup struct), server/internal/httpserver/stripe_webhook.go:134 (LoadOrStore call).

What: SeenSessions sync.Map gets one entry per processed Stripe session and is never trimmed. The comment correctly notes Printful's external_id 409 is the durable backstop across restarts, but for a long-running process this map grows monotonically.

Impact: at 100 orders/day → ~36k entries/year. Each entry is roughly 80 bytes + map overhead (~50 bytes/entry effective). At 1M orders that's tens of MB resident — survivable, but the growth is unbounded. Process restart wipes it.

Recommendation: replace with a fixed-size LRU (e.g., 10k entries) or a TTL map (evict after 24h — Stripe's retry window is 3 days but practical retry storms resolve in minutes). Don't rely on the entry surviving across restarts; the Printful external_id 409 already covers durability.

Effort: small. Could use github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2 (well-tested, single-purpose dep) or hand-roll a small TTL map (no dep). Recommend the latter — the use case is simple enough.

Doesn't need a separate task if it's bundled with TYB-19 (which also touches webhook caching). Consider merging.
